rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SAVINGS DEPOSIT INSURANCE FUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Instructing Clerk To Accept Plaintiffs Request For Entry Of Default Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 13, PETITIONER SAVINGS DEPOSIT INSURANCE FUND , A PUBLIC ENTITY OF THE REPUBLIC OF TURKEY's Motion For Order Instructing Clerk To Accept Plaintiff's Request For Entry Of Default Against Defendant. Denied. Insufficient notice of hearing per CCP 1005. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. FLORAFLEX CORP. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 5, DEFENDANT FLORAFLEX CORP.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Floraflex Corp.'s demurrer to the sole cause of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Keep America Safe and Beautiful (KASB) is overruled. The current demurrer is not untimely. KASB misinterprets the register of actions entry of September 3, 2024. That entry took the prior demurrer off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies and authorized the motion to be re-set after September 19, 2024. The current demurrer complies with that entry. Floraflex's contention that pre-notice is deficient lacks merit. Section VI of the notice directed communications to KASB's counsel, Rebecca M. Jackson, and provided her address and phone number. Floraflex does not explain why KASB's counsel is not a "responsible individual" within the meaning of the Proposition 65 regulations, nor is the court aware of any authority or reason why counsel may not qualify as a responsible individual. Accordingly, Ms. Jackson is a "responsible individual" CCR 25903(b)(2)(A)(1). Floraflex's argument that KASB did not attach a copy of the required summary of Proposition 65 with its notice also fails. The absence of a summary on the Office of the Attorney General's website does not show that KASB failed to append the summary when it mailed notice to Floraflex. Other attachments listed in the notice are also omitted from the website. The proof of service states that the summary was served by mail to Floraflex's CEO. Absent directly controverting evidence, the presumption of Evidence Code 641 governs. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS F. CARLSON VS. SARAH J. BEARSE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 9, DEFENDANT SARAH BEARSE'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's first amended complaint is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts showing malice to support punitive damages. "[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299 (citation omitted); see also College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 713). In paragraph 23 of the first Amended complaint plaintiff alleges that Rico had bitten two other individuals before biting plaintiff. Paragraphs 24, 27 and 57 allege that defendant had knowledge of these prior attacks which posed a threat to the public, yet defendant continued to let Rico roam off leash. These allegations are sufficient to defeat a pleading motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 10, DEFENDANT HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC. Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendant's motion to vacate the default entered on October 23, 2024 is granted per CCP 473(b). The motion is timely as defendant filed it a mere two days after entry of the default. Defendant demonstrates a reasonable excuse. Defendant mistakenly or inadvertently mis-calendared the due date for its response to the complaint. (Ray Decl., par. 10.) There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits (see McClain v. Kissler (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 399, 413) and "very slight evidence will be required to justify the court in setting aside the default." (Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 696.) Defendant therefore meets the requirements for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). Defendant shall file its proposed answer by December 2, 2024. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SVES GO, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 12, CROSS-DEFENDANT THE GAP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the cross-complaint is overruled. Cross-defendant fails to show that the settlement agreement bars this action. Even if the court takes judicial notice of the existence of the agreement, there is a dispute regarding its enforceability. That disputed issue cannot be resolved on demurrer. (Gould v. Maryland Sound Industries, Inc. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4 th 1137 [court could not take judicial notice of written agreement that allegedly barred oral agreement].) A demurrer is " 'simply not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts.' " (Joslin v. H.A.S. Insurance Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 369, 374.) The court accepts cross-complainant's allegations of fraud, duress, and lack of consideration as true. The demurrer to causes of action five and six for tortious interference sufficiently alleges "wrongful conduct" outside the bounds of healthy competition. (Della Penna v. Toyota Motor Sales, U.S.A., Inc. (1995) 11 Cal.4th 376, 393; Cross-Complaint, pars. 8, 12, 15, 17.) The cross- complaint also sufficiently alleges a violation of the UCL. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES BALLARD VS. OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2024, Line 11, 1- DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to all nine causes of action in the complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. CCP 425.10(a)(1) requires that a complaint "shall contain[a] statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." Plaintiff's complaint is totally conclusory and does not plead ultimate facts to support any of the causes of action. Although plaintiff alleges that defendant(s) engaged in discrimination, harassment, retaliation, and other illegal conduct, his complaint does not plead any ultimate facts regarding the employment relationship, plaintiff's alleged protected activity, workplace safety violations, or essential matters The complaint does not even clearly allege that moving defendant was plaintiff's employer. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead facts to support his claims. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
